channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Donald Trump vows to help ‘troubled’ Hollywood with Mel Gibson, Jon Voight and Sylvester Stallone
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Donald Trump wants to make Hollywood “bigger, better and stronger” and has cast Mel Gibson, Jon Voight and Sylvester Stallone as stars of what he is calling his “Special Ambassadors to a great but very troubled place, Hollywood, California.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR and JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: What to know about RedNote, a TikTok alternative
item.description: TikTok patrons are turning to a new platform in light of the nationwide ban set to shut down the app on Sunday. RedNote, a Chinese short-form video platform, is gaining ground as users look for an alternative to the social media company owned by the China's ByteDance.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: College students charged in TikTok-inspired 'catch a predator' plot appear in court
item.description: WORCESTER, Mass. (AP) — Five Massachusetts college students appeared in court Thursday, accused of plotting to lure a man to their campus through a dating app and then seizing him as part of a “Catch a Predator” trend on TikTok.
item.dc:creator: KATHY McCORMACK and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Treasury nominee calls for stronger Russia sanctions and Fed independence at confirmation panel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — U.S. Treasury nominee Scott Bessent on Thursday faced sharp questions from both Democrats and Republicans on tax policy, tariffs, China, Russia sanctions and the future of an IRS tax filing system that Republicans have called to be cut.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: American radicalized by ISIS expressed 'excitement' about traveling overseas to support terrorist group: FBI
item.description: A suspected North Carolina ISIS supporter is facing federal charges after he allegedly made plans to travel to Morocco in December 2024 to join the foreign terrorist organization.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Weelicious founder joins with organization founded by KIND Snacks CEO to help LA wildfire victims
item.description: Weelicious founder discusses how the Frontline Impact Project is working with her brand and others to get resources to frontline workers fighting the devastating LA wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Police detain man dressed in fire gear in Pacific Palisades after reported looting
item.description: Editor’s Note: This article has been updated to include new information provided by the L.A. County Sheriff’s Office. LOS ANGELES (KTLA) – Two men, one of whom was dressed as a firefighter, were detained by L.A.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Louisiana governor relocating homeless people ahead of Super Bowl
item.description: Louisiana authorities are clearing homeless encampments around the Caesars Superdome in New Orleans ahead of next month's Super Bowl.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Fire at home of Florida man missing for 2 years could offer investigators clues
item.description: A recent fire destroyed the home of a Florida man who has been missing for nearly two years, deepening the mystery surrounding his disappearance and reigniting the case.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Veteran skier found buried in avalanche on desolate trail by wife using transceiver
item.description: Donald Moden Jr., 57, was caught in a Colorado avalanche on Jan. 7. More than four hours later, his concerned wife found him three feet under the snow.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Inside the monumental effort behind LA’s firefight, from strategy to meals and laundry
item.description: Firefighters from across the North American continent have converged onto Los Angeles to fight the wildfires.
item.dc:creator: Jesse Bedayn, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: TikTok CEO attending Trump inauguration
item.description: TikTok CEO Shou Zi Chew will attend President-elect Donald Trump's inauguration next week, one day after a potential ban of the video-sharing platform could take effect in the U.S., according to a source familiar with the plans.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, WGN 9

item.title: Bob Uecker, colorful comedian and Baseball Hall of Famer, dies
item.description: From his time as a backup catcher to appearances on Johnny Carson's late night show to his more than five decades in the broadcast booth, people far and wide knew Bob Uecker.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, WFLA, NBC

item.title: He left his LA-area home to cover the wildfires. But the flames were barreling toward his front door
item.description: ALTADENA, Calif. (AP) — The warnings began on Saturday, Jan. 4. Our neighbor, a volunteer at the local sheriff's station, texted that we should “batten down the hatches” for a “big windstorm." From there, things moved fast.
item.dc:creator: RYAN PEARSON, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jack Hoffman, the young cancer patient who ran for a famous 2013 touchdown, dies at 19
item.description: Jack Hoffman, the cancer patient who captured the hearts of Nebraska football fans and inspired the nation as a 7-year-old when he ran for a touchdown during the Cornhuskers' 2013 spring game, has died at the age of 19.
item.dc:creator: Eric Olson, Fox 5

item.title: Harbor seal, flamingo at Chicago zoo die from bird flu
item.description: A Harbor Seal and Chilean Flamingo at the Lincoln Park Zoo died as a result of avian influenza last week.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: United Airlines flight returns to O'Hare after hitting coyote during takeoff
item.description: A United Airlines flight from Chicago to Phoenix had to return to O'Hare International Airport on Sunday morning after its landing gear struck a coyote during takeoff.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, ABC 4

item.title: FDA proposes sweeping limits on nicotine in cigarettes
item.description: It's an 11th-hour plan from the Biden administration that will take years to go into effect, if it happens at all.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, ABC 4

item.title: 2025 Grammys to proceed with focus on wildfire relief. Here's what you can expect
item.description: The 2025 Grammy Awards are right around the corner, which means it is time to get those viewing party plans in action. Allow us to help.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden warns of dangers of 'oligarchy' of ultra-rich, 'tech-industrial complex' running country
item.description: President Joe Biden will deliver his farewell address to the nation on Wednesday, days before President-elect Donald Trump returns to the White House.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Man gets 2 years in US prison for aiding pushy effort to get ex-official to return to China
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A man convicted in the first trial highlighting U.S. claims that China harasses its critics overseas was sentenced Wednesday to two years in prison for his role in a creepy campaign to get a former official to return to his homeland.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NY federal judge denies bail for two real estate brokers and their brother in sex trafficking case
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Two luxury real estate brokers and their brother accused of raping dozens of women over two decades will remain in jail to await their trial on sex trafficking charges, a federal judge in New York City ruled Wednesday. Judge Valerie E.
item.dc:creator: LARRY NEUMEISTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Supreme Court wrestles with explosion of online porn
item.description: A majority of the justices suggested the concept of an age-verification requirement could survive First Amendment scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, NewsNation

item.title: Bondi says she won't play politics as attorney general but doesn't rule out probes of Trump foes
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Pam Bondi, Donald Trump's pick for attorney general, sought to reassure Democratic senators Wednesday that her Justice Department would not prosecute anyone for political purposes but also refused to rule out the potential for investigations into adversaries of the Republican presi
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Fire tornadoes pose a threat in California. A fire lab shows how they work
item.description: As if they aren’t already facing enough, firefighters in California also could encounter fire tornadoes — a rare but dangerous phenomenon in which wildfires create their own weather.
item.dc:creator: HOLLY RAMER and PATRICK WHITTLE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Southwest pilot arrested for suspected DUI at Georgia airport
item.description: A Southwest flight was delayed for five hours Wednesday morning due to a pilot being arrested and charged with DUI, officials said.
item.dc:creator: Natasha Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Whoopi Goldberg backs Carrie Underwood inauguration performance
item.description: The comments about Underwood’s inauguration performance are notable coming from Goldberg, who is commonly a harsh critic of Trump.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FBI charges North Carolina man with trying to join ISIS: court docs
item.description: The complaint alleges Alexander Justin White, 29, used his Facebook account to make frequent posts supporting ISIS in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Sockol, NewsNation

item.title: American woman on Paris-to-Boston flight dies in midair
item.description: A passenger died on board during an Air France flight from Paris to Boston on Tuesday despite life-saving efforts performed by a doctor on the plane.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Coors Light is temporarily changing its name: Here's why
item.description: This year, Coors Light is turning the case of the Mondays into a literal case, with limited-edition packaging and designs.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Palisades Fire firefighters taking part in first-of-its-kind cancer study
item.description: The study comes after the International Agency for Research on Cancer officially classified firefighting as a carcinogenic profession.
item.dc:creator: Juliette Smith, Fox 5

item.title: Officials tout Gaza ceasefire, hostage deal as Israel says details still in flux
item.description: Israel and Hamas agreed to pause the devastating war in the Gaza Strip, mediators announced Wednesday, raising the possibility of winding down the deadliest and most destructive fighting between the bitter enemies.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US in plea talks with Mexican cartel kingpin Ismael 'El Mayo' Zambada
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — U.S. prosecutors said Wednesday that they're discussing a potential plea deal with Ismael “El Mayo” Zambada, the long-elusive Mexican drug lord who was arrested last summer and whose son could testify against him if he goes to trial. Assistant U.S.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FDA bans red dye No. 3 from foods: What to know
item.description: U.S. regulators on Wednesday banned the dye called Red 3 from the nation’s food supply, nearly 35 years after it was barred from cosmetics because of potential cancer risk.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Live coverage: Biden's farewell speech comes on heels of raucous confirmation hearings
item.description: President Biden delivered a somewhat grim primetime farewell speech on Wednesday from the Oval Office to mark the final days of his presidency.
item.dc:creator: The Hill staff, WGN 9

item.title: Dementia cases expected to double by 2060: Study
item.description: Dementia cases in the U.S. are expected to double by 2060 when around 1 million Americans are projected to develop the memory-losing condition every year, according to a new study published Monday in the medical journal Nature Medicine.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Traffic into Manhattan drops 7.5% in first week of new toll. That's 43,000 fewer cars a day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A new toll on drivers entering the core of Manhattan brought modest but measurable traffic reductions to New York City’s heavily-gridlocked streets in its first week of operation, according to preliminary data released Monday by the state's transit authority.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
